title: Equality of Civil Liberties across Social Groups Score
subtitle: Data by [V-Dem](#dod:v-dem). Expert estimates of the extent to which social groups — defined by language, ethnicity, religion, race, region, and/or caste — are free from forced labor, have property rights and access to the justice system, and enjoy freedom of movement. Higher scores mean more equality. A score of 0 is approximately the average across all countries and years.
caption:
Equality of Civil Liberties across Social Groups Score

Data by V-Dem. Expert estimates of the extent to which social groups — defined by language, ethnicity, religion, race, region, and/or caste — are free from forced labor, have property rights and access to the justice system, and enjoy freedom of movement. Higher scores mean more equality. A score of 0 is approximately the average across all countries and years.

This choropleth world map displays the Equality of Civil Liberties across Social Groups Score for 2025. The legend ranges from -3 to 3, where red shades indicate lower equality and blue shades indicate higher equality, with a score of 0 representing the approximate average across all countries and years. The darkest blue bands, corresponding to scores near 3, are visible in a cluster of Northern and Western European nations, including Norway, Sweden, Finland, and Iceland. Uruguay and New Zealand also appear in these darkest blue shades. In contrast, the darkest red bands, indicating scores near -3, are concentrated in specific areas such as China and North Korea. Surrounding these extremes, countries in South America, Southern Africa, and parts of Eastern Europe generally sit in the mid-range blue bands, suggesting scores between 0 and 2. Much of North America, Australia, and Western Europe appear in lighter blue shades, placing them above the global average but below the highest tier. Large portions of the Middle East, North Africa, and Central Asia display light orange to peach tones, indicating scores that fall between 0 and -2. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, signifying no data is available for that territory. The visual distribution shows a concentration of high scores in Western democracies and Oceania, while the lowest scores are clustered in East Asia and parts of the Middle East.
